adjudication: receiving-code-review Response Pattern: READ -> UNDERSTAND -> VERIFY -> EVALUATE -> RESPOND -> IMPLEMENT (one item at a time). No performative agreement.

items.finding: Test does not assert the non-owner returns the SAME charge id (the original).
items.verified: Confirmed against the diff - the test only counted PSP charges.
items.resolution: Extended the regression test: collect all 50 response charge ids, assert len(set(ids)) == 1 and it equals the owner's charge id. Re-ran -> still green. Spec reviewer re-reviewed: compliant.

items.finding: await_charge() busy-waits with a fixed sleep and no timeout; a crashed owner hangs the retry forever.
items.verified: Reproduced: killed the owner task after INSERT 'in_progress' -> non-owner looped indefinitely.
items.resolution: Replaced the fixed sleep with condition-based waiting on record status + a 5s bounded deadline; on deadline a stale 'in_progress' older than the lease is reclaimable (re-runs reserve_or_get). Added a test for the crashed-owner path. Green.
items.order: Important, done after the spec fix

items.resolution: Logged as follow-up PAY-4823 (key-reuse-with-different-body 422). Not blocking this duplicate-charge fix; YAGNI for the page.

items.from: quality (implied 'be safe, add a global lock')
items.finding: Suggestion floated in review thread: 'just take a global lock around the charge path to be safe.'
items.verified: Checked: throughput impact + it is per-process, so it would not even serialize across pods.
items.resolution: Declined with technical reasoning: the per-key atomic INSERT already serializes correctly at the shared store; a global lock would serialize ALL charges (throughput cliff) and still not be cross-process. Pushback accepted.

note: Two real fixes applied + one reasoned pushback + one deferred Minor. Stated as fixes, not 'you're absolutely right'.
source: receiving-code-review (verify before implementing; push back with technical reasoning; YAGNI) + requesting-code-review (act by severity)
